reviews.review_text:
[h1]Borderlands 3[/h1]

still fun as remember when i played it on the ps4 (pre-ordered at the time) honestly i don't get why it's hated so much. yes the dialogues can be corny sometimes and the main campaign have some weak missions that after the first time becomes boring/heavy to replay but it's not so bad as people proclaim, also you can skip it if you own the dlc's and honestly they improve the base game by a mile i suggest to get all of them anyway especially when they are on sale. the gameplay is still incredibly smooth and fun as i remembered the guns feels incredible and the loot is rewarding and interesting to chase a good looter-shooter experience and i really love this genre :P. a little note performance wise the game runs well if you use optimized settings otherwise in heavy chaotic scenes the fps drops will become noticable, and sometimes the games do little stutters but besides this it runs greatly no big complains (bit sad that you can't lower particle effects unless you use mods or edit the game files).

[b]MY PERSONAL DLC'S ENJOYMENT LIST[/b]
played all of them back to back for the first time

- Bounty of Blood
- Psycho Krieg and the Fantastic Fustercluck
- Moxxi's Heist of the Handsome Jackpot
- Designer's Cut 
- Guns, Love, and Tentacles
- Director's Cut

some of the best FPS gameplay ever made but overshadowed by:
- attrocious 'coroporate-edgy' dialogue and voice acting that caused me to play with the voices muted or nearly muted depending who is talking,
- constant unskippable in-game 'cutscenes' with no player interaction that force you to sit there and wait for a few minutes to continue playing,
- uninteresting storyline, sidequests, and characters,
- visually dull, repetitive, linear overworld that is broken up into load-screen gated maps
- content is spread out in ways that encourage frequent fast travel which has high loading times even with a top of the line 2026 PC.

I think the gameplay is good enough to enjoy with youtube or something else playing in the background and the voices off but I wouldn't recommend spending money on such a mixed bag of a game.

好游戏，但我不推荐Take-Two Interactive旗下任何一款游戏。
Good game but I do not recommend any game published by Take-Two Interactive.

2025年2月28日，Take-Two更新了旗下所有游戏的EULA。2025年6月。
On February 28, 2025, Take-Two updated its EULA for all titles.

新协议条款包括：收集设备标识符、IP地址、精准地理位置、支付信息（含信用卡/借记卡号）、网站和应用浏览记录、账户凭证，并获取系统根目录访问权限；美国及澳大利亚、瑞士、英国、欧洲经济区以外地区的用户须放弃集体诉讼和陪审团审判权利，一切争议通过有约束力的个人仲裁解决；禁止使用修改器、MOD、漏洞利用，禁止使用VPN、代理服务器、虚拟机伪装位置或居住地。协议适用于Take-Two旗下所有游戏。
The updated terms include: collection of device identifiers, IP addresses, precise geolocation, payment information (including credit/debit card numbers), web and app browsing history, account credentials, and root-level access to the system directory; users in the U.S. and regions outside Australia, Switzerland, the U.K., and the EEA must waive class action and jury trial rights, resolving all disputes through binding individual arbitration; and the prohibition of mods, cheats, exploits, VPNs, proxies, and virtual machines used to disguise location or residence. These terms apply to all Take-Two games.

我只推荐你在无法获取盗版的情况下购买。
I only recommend purchasing if you cannot obtain a pirated copy.
the story is a hit or miss at points BUT the gameplay is solid

Nice gameplay-wise, but the story falls super short 4 me. Lost it's magic compared to B2.

The DLC hold up though and are superior in terms of storytelling. 

The main antagonists are lame af. 

The CEO is a funny man...
Some of the invasion of privacy stuff is mind boggling. 

Other than that had fun 

Base game 6,0/10, DLC 8,5/10

Personally, this is were the saga dies for me. Gameplay wise this one is the best, guns feel amazing and although I still think Vault Hunters from TPS are still the best bunch, this ones are fun and varied (even if still more than half the skills are just percentage boosts). But everything else goes from meh to terrible.

The story should work, the Vault Hunters will travel to different planets hunting Vaults and facing a deadly cult... you say it and it sounds cool, but the excecution is the worst version you could imagine of that and then some. The spaceship is the worst HUB I´ve seen in any game, and you are forced to walk it and go back and forth to it constantly. The different planets feel pointless, we already saw on BL2 that the same planet can have different biomes, even on TPS with just one Moon and one space station felt more varied than this. And the villains are just... what can I even said it hasn´t being said over the years? Terrible story.

The worst part is that even though most people always claimed nobody played BL games for the story, I was fond of the universe and characters, but here they assasinated every single one. It´s not even the comedy, that was always cringe and BL2 DLCs already went to the moon and beyond over bad humor, it´s even when they try to be serious they also fail miserably, it doesn´t matter what emotion the game tries to produce, it produces nothing.

To some extend the DLCs kind of save the game, they are a mix bag but still have more positive moments than the main campaign. Moxxi Heist it´s the best for people familiar with the IP, it kind of closes some plots but sadly it will remind you that Jack is gone and that they have no idea how to make another great villain. Guns and Tentacles it´s fine, perhaps too much times just waiting for dialogue to end. Bounty and blood it´s very boring storywise but has some good side bosses and extend combat areas. And psycho Krieg DLC is basically Claptrap DLC from the TPS but with a less emotional story and with some of the worst side quests in this entire franchise.

My only possitves beyond gameplay it´s about the leveling system finally allowing you to get to level cap without having to repeat the game over and over, that´s nice, cause previously after level 30 you had to go into VHM or you wouldn´t level up more and then at 60 to TVHM which was completely broken and rather hardcore if you were playing solo. But like I said, too many new negatives and then all the negavites from previous installements are still here and feel even worst, like dialogue completely stalling action, it gets very very annoying and after 10 years and 4 games feels like this devs just think this are normal "features" of the franchise so they keep at it, but if the dialogue isn´t even interesting, it feels like I payed the devs just to make me waste my time.

amazing game for the price usually on sale for only like 3 pounds. have about 100 hours playing this game years ago on a different pc but very much worth the price dont buy if not on sale

pros: 
. guns that feel really good to use
. really good graphic style of the classic borderands cartoonish vibe
. terrible cmedy but thats part of borderlands

cons:
. Ava (doesnt stfu)
. Loreli (doesnt stfu)
. Tyreen (doesnt stfu)
. end game not as much to do as you would hope

Absolute rabid fan of BL 1 and 2.. The Presequel. ehh, not so muc... But in hindsight it wasn't even half bad.
BL3 though.. By the Nine Divines, please just skip it. Don't reward Pitchford for this uninspiring mess.

The jokes are unfunny, it has extremely superfically pandering and generally is an insufferable mess of 'LULZ INTERNETZ CULTURE AMIRITE' cringe, indicating a direction dicated by detached corporate decisions.

Seriously, screw this noise. I wish I hadn't given it the benefit of the doubt and near 50 hours of my life, as I would've asked for a refund because this wasn't worth the full price back in 2020, nor is it the €60 they're STILL asking for it in 2026. Hell I wouldn't even recommend this heap of Bonerfart dung at 50% off.

Does it run well? 
Not bad at the time, but ♥♥♥♥ if I know how well now. Last I tried was 6 years ago and it didn't run half bad, so with anything built after 2020 it probably should do well. It runs on my current rig, but even at character selection I CBA to continue and uninstalled it again. It's that obnoxiously uninspiring.

But is the combat good?
I... guess? If all you want is 'point gun, shoot and loot' then fine, have at it, but I'd still recommend a lobotomy before I'd want to deal with this god awful writing and insipid character design.

Please just go play something else - this horse is closer to glue than another race, yet they keep beating it.

game with forgettable story and gunplay that could be fun but is dragged down to feeling like you're just pointing and clicking things because of how easy it is. It feels like you're going from point a to point b without actually doing anything engaging and it becomes a snooze fest. Also on top of everything it's a disgustingly large game with 130+ gbs 💔💔💔

Borderlands 3 – 9/10 – Bigger, Louder, and Lootier
Borderlands 3 is a massive, bombastic evolution of the looter-shooter formula that delivers more guns, more planets, and more chaos than ever before. Released in 2019 by Gearbox Software, it expands the universe in almost every way: bigger worlds, deeper customization, and an absurd amount of content. The gunplay feels snappier, the new Vault Hunters are a blast to play, and the co-op mayhem reaches new heights. It’s a game that knows exactly what fans want—mountains of loot and nonstop action—and it delivers with style. However, a weaker story and less memorable antagonists keep it from reaching the same legendary heights as its predecessor.
One of the standout features of Borderlands 3 is the refined and ridiculously satisfying gunplay. True to the series, there are billions of guns, each with unique manufacturers, quirks, and elemental effects that make every drop exciting. The new Vault Hunters— the Operative Zane, Beastmaster FL4K, Siren Amara, and Gunner Moze—bring fresh playstyles and skill trees that support wildly different builds. Combat is fast, fluid, and full of verticality, with sliding, mantling, and brutal action skills that let you tear through hordes of psychos and bandits. The moment-to-moment shooting feels better than ever, and the sheer variety keeps the gameplay loop addictive for hundreds of hours.
The campaign takes you beyond Pandora to multiple planets, each with its own distinct biomes and personality. Exploring the neon-drenched Promethea, the eerie Eden-6, and the wasteland of Pandora itself is a visual treat. The story follows the new Vault Hunters as they battle the Calypso Twins and their Children of the Vault cult. While the narrative is serviceable and moves at a brisk pace with plenty of classic Borderlands humor, it lacks the sharp writing, emotional weight, and iconic villainy of Borderlands 2. Handsome Jack is sorely missed, and the Twins never quite reach the same level of memorable menace. Some sections feel bloated or repetitive, and the story beats can be hit-or-miss, which is the main reason this entry doesn’t hit a perfect score.
Where Borderlands 3 truly excels is in its multiplayer. The four-player co-op is seamless and remains one of the best in the genre. Whether you’re farming bosses, completing side quests, or diving into the endgame, playing with friends turns the game into an absolute riot. The endgame is deep and rewarding, with Mayhem modes, Proving Grounds, and a constant stream of new gear to chase. The DLCs are also excellent, adding substantial new storylines, maps, and characters that help round out the experience.
Visually, Borderlands 3 is a big step up. The cel-shaded art style looks more vibrant and detailed than ever, with stunning planetary vistas and chaotic particle effects during big battles. Performance is solid (especially on newer hardware), and the sound design—crisp gunfire, hilarious voice lines, and a killer soundtrack—keeps you immersed throughout.
What holds Borderlands 3 back from a perfect 10 is primarily its story. While the gameplay loop is stronger than ever, the narrative and villains don’t leave the same lasting impression as previous entries. Some pacing issues and occasional repetition in side content also prevent it from feeling quite as tight.
9/10 – Explosive, Entertaining, and Endlessly Replayable
Borderlands 3 is still an outstanding looter-shooter packed with personality, incredible combat, and endless loot-chasing fun. It may not dethrone Borderlands 2 as the series high point due to its story shortcomings, but it remains a must-play for fans of the genre. If you love shooting, looting, and laughing with friends, this is one of the most generous and entertaining games on the market. Highly recommended—just don’t go in expecting the same narrative magic as before.
not really a fan of the writing but the gameplay is great!
The gunplay is great and the gameplay loop is enjoyable. Not much else to say.
Gameplay is super fun, guns are interesting but the story and the characters are horrible. I played it on mute tbh.
Good gameplay some nice characters if you played BL2 and are planning on playing 4 you need to play this, didn't live up to the expectations, but still good game

God I just really hate this game, the dialogue is more dry than anything. The shooting and fire fights feel awkward. I haven't played for that long but god do I not want to play anymore. It's like its being satire making fun of bad games, but instead itself is a bad game. Boring walking sim.
Really fun chaotic and crazy good gameplay love it to bits

This game has great gunplay, amazing graphics and great dlc. But this comes at a cost.. The game feels like a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ chore at times, and all the collectibles, npc's not shutting. And worst of all, the story is so boring and predictable thats I wonder what they were up to. Its so cringe.

Only buy this when its on sale with the pandora box collection for 40 bucks
good gameplay, the story sucks, guns go pew pew good

if your the type of gamer that either A runs modded or B doesn't care for story and only gameplay OR C both you'll likely love this game i know a ton of people have been coming around to this game so if ya love it don't feel bad about it
Going to the end game and grinding for the perfect gears. Absolute fun

I just love the gameplay and I think this game is 100 times better than borderlands 4 because 4 has a lot of new mechanics that I can't memorize all of them and also spawning a car out thin air in 4 is not fun because having to travel a certain distance just to get a car is exilerating.
I love this game. I have played on console and rebought it on Steam. cool thing about on PC I'm able to mod, which is fun. Highly Recommend this game.
Started a new game, but for some reason when I pick up loot it wont show in my bag, so weird....

Claptrap’s still here. 5 stars!

The combat in Borderlands 3 is gripping... there’s no other way to put it. Everything from weapon variety to sound effects, to that specific... PUNCH you feel with each shot, I absolutely love it. There’s something so special about Borderlands’ unique first-person combat; something you can’t quite put your finger on. Maybe it’s the buffs, or elemental shenanigans—but regardless, a combat system that oozes flourish in a game designed around crazy left-field humour and wacky hijinks—no doubt it serves its purpose.

A huge difference between Borderlands and other titles, though, is optionality. You are regularly encouraged to attack, defend, dash, or retreat depending on your playstyle. Unless combat is narratively necessary (e.g.: clearing a room to execute a task within it), you are free to experiment, no pressure to fly in guns-blazing all the time.

Sometimes I enjoy sprinting and sliding from cover-to-cover to sneak past defences; that enhancement to player freedom is genius design-wise. Plus, easing the grip on the player rapidly shifts engagement. There are so many points in BL3 where you’re told to shoot... so, damn, many... therefore, tackling it my own way firmly glued me to the screen. Otherwise, I’d have been free of my stamina after three shootouts and called it a day.

Plot-wise, in honesty, my investment was thin. That’s fine, right? If a story’s not for you, it’s cool... though, it’s harder to defend a game when "thin" is exactly what you enjoyed about it. I wasn’t sprinting to the next objective, but I was engaged enough to keep going, and that was plenty for me.

I understood the goal, the motivations. That, alongside amazing character dynamics and stellar voice acting... the plot carries itself forward. Besides, BL3 is naturally more character-focused than narrative-driven compared to its predecessors—so they definitely weaselled their way out of that one. Or... skagged, their way out of it?

To “build” on this (get it?)... is the world-building.
The introduction of different planets made the world of Borderlands feel deeply alive—even as each planet you visit is crumbling in real time. From Pandora to Eden-6, they are all distinct and serve a unique narrative or structural purpose with new enemies, strategies, and secrets that instil meaning through every crash landing.

It’s also stunning by the way! The sheer variety of colour schemes one game can use, especially so effectively, is weirdly inspiring. I’m in awe of these worlds and how expansive they feel; how perfectly they embody the archetype they’re meant to represent. It’s an added bonus on the surface, but that touch of beauty contrasts with the destruction within, allowing us a sight to behold; one that’s slipping away by the second.... it’s really good, if you couldn’t tell.

Characters! The character building in Borderlands 3, as I mentioned, is its centre stage. Through the way they all act, there’s an undercurrent of understanding that this is totally where all the effort went—as they’re all just so lovable in different ways. The combination of harsh, ridiculous, detached, and charming introduces so many character interactions you simply don’t want to miss.

It’s a cacophony of voices, all fumbling to speak louder than the rest, leading to so many hilarious moments of banter that intimately connects the player to the characters. And with how natural it feels, it’s only fate I begin to love this game even more!

A quick point here, but the reveals were... shocking, believe it or not.
I feel like I should’ve seen them coming—so I may just be an idiot... but regardless—to see myself constantly blown away is something I’ll always cherish. Defining factor or not, it really resonated with me. So please, keep doing crazy ♥♥♥♥ I can’t predict!

Unfortunately, a huge complaint—betraying my central point here a bit—but some characters are incredibly annoying and, begrudgingly, cringe. You meet Vaughn around 10 minutes in, then you meet Rhys... then you meet Tyreen. It’s a lot.

As much as the characters can be hilariously charming, they can be equally hilariously frustrating. I suppose it comes with the territory of having this “quirky” style of writing, but most of the dialogue is mad edgy, usually boiling down to “get it? Because I’m evil and I kill people” or “get it? Because I’m gross and ugly” which gets frustrating after a while.

Overall, Borderlands 3 is definitely a guilty pleasure, and I’m saying that now barely a day after finishing it. The flaws arrive in droves, you struggle with the pros, yet somehow come out the other side loving it. I treasure that kind of game, though, so we’re a perfect match!

8.5/10—and yes, the extra 0.5 is absolutely necessary.
It does a lot of heavy lifting, just like Tiny Tina singlehandedly making this game a masterpiece. Mind your steel-plated doors, though. You might think they’re indestructible, but she’ll blast right through them calling it a “pizza delivery.”

As someone thats put quite alot of time and effort into this franchise on PS3/ PS4, I have to say they stepped up their game.
Yeah the dialogue is cringe and it reads like a tumblr fan wrote it but overall, best gun and gameplay of the series so far.

If only they could have made a sequel with more recent hardware and software, I would definitely buy the next game that Gearbox made after this one.

If you're going to pick this game up to play at all, I highly recommend two things that will make this much more enjoyable.

1. Get a friend or two to play with, makes even the base campaign that much more enjoyable.

2. Get all the DLC as the main game itself (the story especially) falls flat.

This goes on sale quite often so that's the time I would pick this up. The gun play and movement controls are the highlight of this game by far (nothing will come close to Borderlands 2 for me personally.)

Not the best in the series, but, especially at its completed state, not the worst by far.

The main story is abysmally bad, but it is undoubtably the best gameplay and gunplay in the series. It feels so good to run around and shoot stuff. All the characters are unique and fun to play. While legendaries are far more plentiful than any other game in the series, there are so many cool and fun ones with amazing synergies. The soundtrack is amazing, Jesper Kyd and Raison Varner crushed it. The Maliwan Takedown alone is better than anything BL4 can put out and that's only ONE of the FREE dlcs they added. The Halloween event is amazing, the Cartel event is amazing. This game is so good it makes me sad to see how BL4 ended up.

Its my first boderlands game.
10/10 for action
10/10 for story
0/10 for driving
worth it.
Would play again. After a while I got used to this game, but I hated the look of it at first. It's still a great solution if you're looking for another Bordelands game to play.

Great gameplay
Good balance of graphics to performance. At least for a game like this (im looking at you bl4)
very fun game but it feels like the design department for the guns this time around were seriously off their rocker purely from a looks perspective like why do hyperion guns look like that
similar to the far cry series - gameplay is good, but the rest is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥

best game in the series by: gameplay, systems, dlc, clicking on things and watching them get blasted to bits. worst game in the series by: main story and getting legos handed to you like tictacs at a diabetic's convention. an absolute steal on a sale tho, runs miles better than it's younger brother bl4

Don't know why people didn't like the game at first. Nothing is wrong with it. I've played bl 1, 2, 3 with zero issues. Story is great in all of them. Humor is the same in all of them, perhaps even more over the top in 3. It's just a nice silly little looter shooter with a dumb story.
fantastic game. the game after the ending however is not worth playing. just not engaging

Borderlands 3 is like a Butter Chicken wrap with too much sugar in it. 

At least Rhys and Vaughn are in Borderlands 3 which made Borderlands 3 a bit better! 

It’s honestly hard to give Borderlands 3 a thumbs up or a thumbs down because I don’t hate it but I don’t like it for its bad written story. 

Maya was cool in BL2 and in BL3 but then they decided to just disrespect Maya by killing her off with a horrible death and it’s all because that disgraceful little ♥♥♥♥ Ava didn’t listen to her! 

Maya didn’t deserve to die that is not okay! 

Ava blames Lilith for the death of Maya I shot Ava in the face over 80 times the whole time she was blaming Lilith and shortly after that I continuously shoot Ava in the face even more! 

So basically before Ava was going to directly blame Lilith I shot Ava in the face and killed Ava! 

I’m glad Ava is not in Borderlands 4! 

Why Ava is not in Borderlands 4 that’s because I shot Ava in the face so she’s dead! 

The sirens that died in Borderlands 3 Ava, Maya, Troy, Tyreen 

So 4 siren characters got killed in Borderlands 3! 

Who got to lead the Crimson Raiders is Amara! 

So in the ending of Borderlands 3 Amara is the one who is the new leader of the Crimson Raiders and Sanctuary 3 belongs to Amara! 

At some point in the future Fiona Dillon will be a playable vault hunter in a future Borderlands game! 

I just wish there’s a middle thumb option for reviewing a game! 

Borderlands 3 is a 9.2 out of 10 because it is a fun game. 

I really look forward to seeing more Fiona and Sasha and we really want more Fiona and Sasha! 

More Fiona and Sasha is what I really want!

It's great fun and delivers the mayhem like only the Borderlands franchise can. Is it the same quality of writing as BL2? No. Is it still compelling? Yes. And you can't get better than Moze's Iron Bear!
all other bs aside my character got corrupted for essentially no reason quite funny that a triple AAA game cant be ported very well hope your ceo dies
the game is slightly better than 2 in the engine and movement as well as gun play and in my opinion character builds but lacks in story elements but also contains a larger more fleshed out map

Personally I like Borderland 2 more than any other Borderlands, but this one is really good. Combat is good I love the unique weapons and reload animations. The cosmetics and characters are really good after every mission I spend about 10 minutes making a new outfit. The most important part of any Borderlands game is the comedy.  The comedy is funny and not knowing what Claptrap is gonna say out of the blue. Over all Borderlands 3 is worth it especially if you liked the first two.
Good game based on overall improvements from previous games.  The Story is what lets this one down however.
Gets better once you get used to the changes and become familiar with the overall game.

Borderlands 3 is honestly a great game if you enjoy chaotic looter shooters with tons of guns, abilities, and experimentation.
The graphics still look excellent, the gameplay is smooth, and there is almost always something to do. Even after finishing the main story, I immediately started a harder mode because the game still stays enjoyable once you understand the mechanics better.
What I enjoy most is that the game rewards experimentation. You can accidentally discover crazy weapon/shield combinations and weird synergies without even trying to exploit the game. Sometimes you end up feeling completely overpowered just from mixing the right gear together naturally, which makes the gameplay really fun.
Yes, every Borderlands game follows a similar formula, but if you personally enjoy the style, humour, gunplay, and loot system, it becomes very addictive in a good way.
The game runs extremely well on my system and still feels modern visually. Definitely worth it if you enjoy action-heavy games with lots of replay value.
Came from BL4, weird way to start the franchise (in reverse) but this game feels way more polished than 4. Only annoying thing is the lack of a boss respawn machine
Plenty of content in this game, more so than 1 or 2. Recommend getting the dlc package when on sale for even more hours of fun. I enjoyed this story line the most so far.

Fantastic gameplay, smooth, clean, crisp shooting, very responsive, very satisfying. 

The worst story in any game I've ever played. I consider this game's story to be non-canon. Entire characters are just inexplicably rewritten and their personalities are unrecognizable. New characters are introduced that are non-descript and utterly forgettable. Everyone is made so. Damn. Annoying. Half the voice actors seemed to just phone it in (looking at you, Ice Cube). The jokes feel constructed in a lab; you can tell that the pieces of a joke are present, but nothing more. The jokes in this game would get a completion grade in a scriptwriting 101, but not much else. If you think Sausage Party is the funniest movie ever written, you'll probably love this game's story. 

Story is incomprehensible against the backdrop of the Borderlands universe. Just makes no sense at all, I'm sorry. What's worse is the canon integrity wasn't even sacrificed for a good reason. The writers deviated from the story to make something so, so much worse. As stated before certain characters essentially have their Normal versions, and then their weird BL3 versions (Hammerlock is among the worst offenders of this). 

Maps are gorgeous. Guns are beautifully made. I enjoy being able to see the parts on a gun within the game (BL2 fans remember memorizing what each manufacturer's parts look like). This game did a lot right. Hell, it did most things right. The entire writing staff just needs fired immediately and blacklisted from any AAA office into perpetuity. 

Worth playing. Just turn dialogue volume to 0, disable subtitles, and follow the diamonds for the story missions. That's much better than listening to Ava for even one minute.

This game is awesome! I love the gameplay and different skill trees that actually feel impactful. Borderlands 2 is much better storywise, but Borderlands 3 still offers an interesting story and wonderful shoot-and-loot experience. Blasting enemies in the face is so much more enjoyable in this game.
i love the gameplay even tho i am writing this too early just wanted to say that this game is super super fun and i reccomebd this game to everyone cause this is a fun game
This game is fun. The writing sucks. Games are more about fun gameplay than good writing, so if you would rather play a game with phenomenal writing then I recommend you read a book
The gameplay is quite amazing but Good GOD! Listening to some of those lines is pure TORTURE! I feel like I should be compensated for putting up with this Millennial-ASS-writing.

garbage game really, good graphics, sometimes funny jokes and fl4k is fun gunplay is nice but besides that empty baron of wasteland everything about the game feels empty tiring that open world slop no one asked for and a chore to do apprently game gets better after the story ends but what let upto all of that around 40 hours of gameplay was so not fun half the jokes are just about sexual themes that a pervert wrote not to even be funny just to put it in game or about 5th grader jokes you won't end up liking it if your not a child playing the game and killing someone doesnt feel like it's worth anything, any reason that be and then the game expects u to be sad about a character dying i regret buying this game even though it was 6 dollars  nothing about this game hit me well not the bosses not the system anything really probably goes at a list of the worst games ive played do what you can with this info

fanbase gotta be a group of just piggies cause this is just pure slop lol

In my opinion this is the best Borderlands game of 1,2,3, and 4. That said, the villians (Calypso Twins) and the especially the brat little girl are SUPER ANNOYING, you have to just turn your volume down. The rest of the game is great (not perfect).
Better and more interesting than the fourth part, so diligently pushed, which was not particularly difficult to finish.
This game is generational if you have Spotify in the background and mute the dialogue.
The game is full of crashes and bugs!

I'm pretty late to the gaming industry and playing Borderlands. I bought the Pandoras Box and thought I was getting an awesome deal. I first played BL2 and got hooked, I wanted to play others so here I am today. BL3 is so stupidly written and the way it plays compared to BL1,2, and pre sequel. The tales of and new tales of are not even games, so that was disappointing also. The first 3 are great to play and the story draws you in, minus BL2s DLC fight for sanctuary. Not a fan of that one either, bad story that makes no sense. BL3 has you chasing a key that so called commander Lilith has lost twice. First in Fight for Sanctuary DLC in BL2 and then after you fight the bad guys to get it back in the begging of BL3. The characters talk way too much and their so called humor is anything other than humor. None of the weapons work as good as the other games, they function horribly. In this game you are shooting baby looking characters, Punky Brewster type girls. Sir Hammerlock is now gay and wanting to get married. You travel to a planet that is someone's mind. You're playing on tv shows for gladiator type entertainment. I am still playing it to finish the game, I hope it gets better but I don't see how. I don't think anyone needs a commentary on how abysmal the campaign and many side quest story lines are. This is known information at this point.
Aside from this, bosses are designed well, have well thought out mechanics that make them enjoyable to fight.
Mayhem mode is a mixed bag, it can add sufficient complexity for those looking for a somewhat chaotic experience, though I often find myself playing with it disabled or on mayhem 11 to ignore the modifiers. Locking some drops behind mayhem mode serves to push players into it, which is probably fine, but I can see it rubbing some the wrong way. Build diversity also takes a nose dive after some mayhem levels as certain weapons simply scale better, how big a deal this is will depend on the person in particular. You can technically make anything work, it will just take years to kill anything.
The legendary designs for the most part are fantastic, featuring some returning items and plenty of new ones. I like a lot of them.
Level design is not super condensed, I feel it could have used a smaller map in several locations, but for the most part it isn't egregious and can be traversed without too much effort, though it does feel like many areas call for a car. (or a good snowdrift/speed zane build)
The fps elements are vastly improved compared to BL2, it is fun to simply move around in the environments and shot the guns give to you, regardless of whether or not they are legendary. Sliding and mantling are not revolutionary, but they feel satisfying to use.
The characters are all unique in their own ways and have varied playstyles, I have completed entire playthroughs of each except flak. And can safely say that they are all very fun in their own ways, and have several viable ways to build their skill trees.

So to summarize, this game has a lot of positives with one GLARING negative. The story and humor is dated at best, and downright lazy if given proper attention. This can be remedied by getting a dialogue skip and speed up mod to allow you to completely remove this negative. After you remove these, you have a very fun game to play that has a good endgame with proper content to "test your mettle" against. I would recommend this game for this reason.

aight listen up, i played borderlands 2 a bunch like.... a long time ago, and i remember the dialogues as witty and genuinely entertaining. I'm not sure what happened, maybe i matured and became detached from what used to give me joy, but I don't find claptrap funny in the slightest (mind you, BL2 he was my favorite yapper)

The 2nd thing that bothers me is the NPCs don't shut up, it's like they all went to get their masters in yaponomics and are fighting for who can occupy comms the longest, once you notice how much they ramble on and on you kind of can't unsee it anymore.

Gameplay-wise - good game, it's fun, the variety in weapons and types and all that shabang is great, you got several different builds, you can honestly get a lot of hours out of the game and i just recently finished the main story and im replaying it on the harder difficulty.

But yeah, basically the BL2 was charming because the humor was good, the dialogue was snappy and the overall feel of combat was great, we're just left with the last point nowadays

MY REVIEW WAS REMOVED BY MODERATORS FROM BORDERLANDS 4, THIS IS A BORDERLANDS 4 REVIEW!!!
First time installing and i can't even play with a friend which is also their fist time installing because we're on "incompatible versions" somehow.... horrible. Randy Dihshort 
:UPDATE For anyone who runs into this issue
1. Navigate to C:\Users\YOURNAME\Documents\My Games\Borderlands 4\Saved\PersistentDownloadDir
2. Check inside that folder for a "Gearbox" and "shiftsdk" folder. If you have both of these folders zip the whole folder "PersistentDownloadDir"
3. Send the PersistentDownloadDir.zip to the friend who does NOT have these folders. (I used gmail.)
       This is the fix that worked for me. Apparently this has been an issue since launch....

BL3 was great. I particularly liked the co-op, and the voice acting. Ice T was hilarious as the navigator! I also liked Penn and Teller. And the mustached capitalist guy had me ROTF as well. 

It's a SOLID shooter. Just wish I could swap 6 guns as I never really used 2 guns in combat. The gun variety was FANTASTIC! I also liked the option to resurrect after dying. 

Would have been nice to be able to increase the difficulty before finishing the main campaign. It was a bit easy if you have the best available gear.

Also, the last boss in the main campaign made me really think about matching personalities with outcomes. Some personality types have such horrible endings. You see that in life too if you live long enough. Really thoughtful moment.

Important information contained in the Terms of Service:

Mods may lead to account suspension.

Displaying cheats or exploiting glitches may also result in a ban.

Mandatory arbitration clause and waiver of the right to class action and jury trial for all users residing in the United States and any other territory except Australia, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, or countries of the European Economic Area.

Using a VPN when connecting to online servers may result in a ban.

Accessing game content through a virtual machine is prohibited.

Types of data collected:

Identifiers / Contact Information: Name, username, gamertag, postal and email address, phone number, unique IDs, mobile device ID, platform ID, game service ID, advertising ID (IDFA, Android ID), and IP address.

Protected Characteristics: Age and gender.

Commercial Information: Purchase and usage history, including gameplay information and preferences.

Payment Information: Payment details (credit/debit card) and... Delivery address

Internet Activity / Electronic Activity: Web/application browsing, gameplay data related to the services, online interactions with the services and advertising, information about the games and platforms you use, and data about installed applications

Device and Usage Data: Device type, software and hardware information, language settings, browser type and version, operating system, and data on how users interact with the services (e.g., content viewed, pages accessed, clicks, scrolling)

Profile Inferences: Inferences made based on your information and web activity to create a personalized profile and identify products and services that may interest you

Audio/Visual Information: Profile photos, account images and avatars, audio data collected through chat features, gameplay recordings, and video images (such as in game tests)

Sensitive Information: Precise location (if you allow access), account credentials (username and password), and content of communications made via chat
